content.rendered:
This article is all about insects. First, you can find the names of some common Finnish insects and some words related to their environment, behavior and more. Lastly, I explain some Finnish sayings related to insects!

Finnish English
hyönteinen insect
ötökkä bug
kärpänen fly
hyttynen / sääski mosquito
perhonen butterfly
ampiainen wasp
mehiläinen bee
kimalainen bumblebee
paarma horsefly
kovakuoriainen beetle
leppäkerttu ladybug
sudenkorento dragonfly
heinäsirkka grasshopper
muurahainen ant
hämähäkki spider
mato worm
punkki tick
kirppu flea
täi louse
lude bedbug
torakka cockroach
satiainen groin louse
Finnish English
loinen parasite
syöpäläiset vermin
toukka larva
verkkosilmät compound eyes
siipi wing
pölyttäjä pollinator
munia to lay eggs
parvi swarm
kuningatar queen
kuhnuri drone
siitepöly pollen
tuntosarvet antennae
mehiläisyhdyskunta bee colony
mehiläispesä beehive
hunajakenno honeycomb
parveilu swarming
muurahaiskeko ant hill

Finnish Sayings Related to Insects

  • “Hän ei tee pahaa kärpäsellekään.”
    • Literally: He doesn’t do bad even to flies.
    • Meaning: He’s harmless, really nice.
  • “Älä tee kärpäsestä härkästä.”
    • Literally: Don’t make a fly into a “härkänen” (word only used in this saying, related to “härkä” (bull))
    • Meaning: Don’t make a mountain out of a mole hill.
  • “Olisi ollut kiva olla kärpäsenä katossa tarkkailemassa.”
    • Literally: It would have been nice to watch as a fly from the ceiling.
    • Meaning: I’d have liked to look in undetected.
  • “Minulla on perhosia vatsassa.”
    • Literally: I have butterflies in my stomach.
    • Meaning: I’m excited/nervous.
